exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.description: Hip-hinge compound lift pulling a loaded barbell from the floor to standing, building posterior chain strength and total-body power.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description: Hip-hinge with a slight knee bend, lowering the barbell along the legs to maximise hamstring and glute stretch under load.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
exercises.description: Wide-stance deadlift with toes angled out, placing greater demand on the glutes and inner thighs than conventional stance.

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.description: Foundational lower-body movement with a barbell on the upper back, driving through the heels to develop quad and glute strength.

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.description: Squat variation with the barbell held in a front rack position, emphasising the quadriceps and demanding significant core stability.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.description: Squat holding a kettlebell at chest height, promoting an upright torso and deep range of motion for lower-body development.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: Seated machine movement pushing a weighted platform away, allowing heavy quad loading with spinal support throughout the set.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.description: Seated machine exercise extending the knee against resistance to isolate and directly strengthen the quadriceps muscle group.

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.description: Lying or seated machine movement curling the heels toward the glutes under resistance to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings.

exercises.name: Calf Raise
exercises.description: Plantarflexion movement on a machine or step, targeting the gastrocnemius and soleus for lower leg size and strength.

exercises.name: Lunge
exercises.description: Step forward or backward under dumbbell load, lowering the back knee to build unilateral leg strength and hip stability.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.description: Rear-foot elevated split squat with dumbbells, driving quad and glute hypertrophy through a deep unilateral range of motion.

exercises.name: Flat Bench Press
exercises.description: Horizontal barbell press on a flat bench, the primary compound chest builder for upper-body pushing strength and muscle mass.

exercises.name: Incline Bench Press
exercises.description: Barbell press on an inclined bench, shifting emphasis to the upper chest and anterior deltoids for a complete chest development.

exercises.name: Decline Bench Press
exercises.description: Barbell press on a declined bench targeting the lower chest fibres with reduced anterior shoulder involvement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.description: Wide-arc dumbbell fly on a flat bench, stretching and contracting the pectoral fibres through a full chest-focused range of motion.

exercises.name: Cable Fly
exercises.description: Cable crossover movement maintaining constant tension throughout the chest fly arc, providing superior pectoral isolation and stretch.

exercises.name: Push Up
exercises.description: Fundamental bodyweight pressing movement building chest, shoulder, and tricep strength and endurance with no equipment required.

exercises.name: Dip
exercises.description: Parallel bar dip lowering the body by flexing the elbows, targeting chest and triceps depending on forward torso lean angle.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.description: Standing barbell press directly overhead, building deltoid strength and shoulder stability while demanding significant core engagement.

exercises.name: Arnold Press
exercises.description: Dumbbell press with a rotational movement through the range, hitting all three deltoid heads for complete shoulder development.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Unilateral dumbbell raise to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend, isolating the medial deltoid for shoulder width.

exercises.name: Front Raise
exercises.description: Dumbbell raise directly in front to shoulder height, targeting the anterior deltoid with strict form and controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.description: Cable pull toward the face with a rope attachment, targeting the rear deltoids and external rotators to improve shoulder health.

exercises.name: Rear Delt Fly
exercises.description: Bent-over dumbbell lateral raise targeting the posterior deltoids and upper back for balanced shoulder and postural development.

exercises.name: Pull Up
exercises.description: Overhand bodyweight pull to a fixed bar, one of the best compound movements for lat width and overall upper back development.

exercises.name: Chin Up
exercises.description: Underhand grip bodyweight pull-up that increases bicep involvement alongside lat and upper back activation for strength gains.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: Cable pulldown to the chest using a wide or neutral grip, building lat width and creating the V-tapered back appearance.

exercises.name: Seated Row
exercises.description: Horizontal cable row from a seated position, targeting the mid-back and biceps with full scapular retraction at contraction.

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.description: Hinge-position barbell row pulling toward the lower chest, building upper back thickness and significant bilateral bicep strength.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.description: Unilateral dumbbell row braced on a bench, allowing heavy load and full scapular retraction for back thickness development.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.description: Landmine or machine T-bar row in a bent-over position, emphasising middle back thickness and density with heavy loading.

exercises.name: Shrug
exercises.description: Shoulder elevation with a loaded barbell to isolate the upper trapezius for increased upper back and neck region development.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.description: Standing supinated barbell curl, the fundamental bilateral movement for building bicep mass, strength, and peak development.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.description: Unilateral dumbbell curl allowing wrist supination at the top to fully contract and isolate each bicep independently.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.description: Neutral-grip dumbbell curl targeting the brachialis and brachioradialis for arm thickness and complementary forearm strength.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.description: Barbell curl on a preacher bench that eliminates body momentum, forcing strict bicep isolation through the complete range.

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.description: Standing cable curl providing continuous tension throughout the full range of motion for thorough bicep development.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.description: Downward cable press with rope or bar attachment, isolating all three tricep heads with constant tension through elbow extension.

exercises.name: Skull Crusher
exercises.description: Lying barbell extension lowering the bar toward the forehead, isolating all three tricep heads for mass and pressing strength.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description: Dumbbell or cable extension overhead placing the long head of the tricep under maximal stretch and peak tension.

exercises.name: Tricep Dip
exercises.description: Narrow-stance bench or bar dip with upright torso, targeting the triceps primarily as distinct from chest-focused parallel dips.

exercises.name: Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.description: Narrow-grip barbell bench press that shifts load from the chest to the triceps for compound pressing tricep development.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Static hold in push-up position with the full core braced, an isometric exercise for spinal stability and core endurance.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Lateral isometric hold on one forearm, targeting the obliques and lateral core for anti-lateral flexion stability and strength.

exercises.name: Crunch
exercises.description: Spinal flexion movement curling the upper torso toward the knees to target and isolate the rectus abdominis muscles.

exercises.name: Sit Up
exercises.description: Full-range trunk flexion from lying to upright, training the abdominals and hip flexors together through complete motion.

exercises.name: Leg Raise
exercises.description: Lying hip flexion raising straight legs to vertical, targeting the lower abdominals with minimal equipment and maximum tension.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: Seated rotational movement with feet elevated, targeting the obliques and transverse abdominis through controlled trunk rotation.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.description: Kneeling cable crunch with rope attachment providing progressive overload for rectus abdominis hypertrophy and core strength.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.description: Kneeling rollout with an ab wheel performing an advanced anti-extension movement for building total core strength.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Barbell hip extension with shoulders elevated on a bench, the premier exercise for glute hypertrophy and posterior chain power.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Supine hip extension pressing through the heels to lift the hips, targeting the glutes and hamstrings effectively with bodyweight.

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.description: Barbell hip-hinge from standing with the bar across the back, strengthening the posterior chain and erector spinae under load.

exercises.name: Hyperextension
exercises.description: Back extension on a hyperextension bench targeting the erector spinae, glutes, and hamstrings through controlled hip extension.

exercises.name: Farmer Carry
exercises.description: Walking with heavy dumbbells or handles at the sides, developing grip strength, forearm endurance, and full-body stability.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
exercises.description: Unilateral loaded walk with one dumbbell, demanding lateral core stability and grip strength while resisting lateral trunk flexion.

exercises.name: Battle Ropes
exercises.description: Alternating or simultaneous rope slams building explosive power, cardiovascular fitness, and upper-body muscular endurance.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.description: Explosive jump onto a raised platform developing lower-body power, reactive strength, and neuromuscular coordination.

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.description: Combination squat thrust and jump developing full-body coordination, explosive power, and cardiovascular conditioning simultaneously.

exercises.name: Mountain Climber
exercises.description: Plank-position alternating knee drives at pace, combining core stability with cardiovascular and hip flexor conditioning.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description: Ballistic hip-hinge swinging a kettlebell to shoulder height, training posterior chain power and cardiovascular capacity simultaneously.

exercises.name: Turkish Get Up
exercises.description: Complex unilateral movement from lying to standing with overhead kettlebell, developing mobility, stability, and total-body functional strength.

exercises.name: Clean
exercises.description: Olympic lift pulling the barbell from the floor to the front rack in one explosive movement, developing full-body power.

exercises.name: Power Clean
exercises.description: Explosive Olympic derivative catching the barbell in a quarter-squat position for maximum power output and rate of force development.

exercises.name: Snatch
exercises.description: Full Olympic lift receiving the barbell overhead in one continuous movement from the floor, requiring total-body coordination and explosive power.

exercises.name: Hang Clean
exercises.description: Clean variation starting from the hang position at mid-thigh, focusing the second pull and explosive hip extension pattern.

exercises.name: Push Press
exercises.description: Overhead press using a dip-and-drive leg action to overcome sticking points, loading the shoulders heavier than a strict press.

exercises.name: Thruster
exercises.description: Front squat combined with an overhead press in one fluid movement, a demanding exercise for full-body strength and metabolic conditioning.

exercises.name: Wrist Curl
exercises.description: Seated wrist flexion with a dumbbell resting on the knee, isolating the forearm flexors for grip and wrist strength.

exercises.name: Reverse Curl
exercises.description: Overhand-grip barbell curl targeting the brachioradialis and forearm extensors, complementing traditional supinated curl training.

exercises.name: Zottman Curl
exercises.description: Supinated curl ascending and pronated descending, training both the biceps and brachioradialis together in one efficient movement.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Single-arm cable lateral raise providing constant tension throughout the full arc for superior medial deltoid isolation.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.description: Guided horizontal press on a chest machine allowing heavy loading with joint stability for chest and tricep hypertrophy.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Guided overhead press machine for shoulder development with a fixed movement path, reducing injury risk while maintaining high intensity.

exercises.name: Machine Row
exercises.description: Chest-supported or seated machine row with a fixed path, allowing heavy back loading without the fatigue of maintaining a hinge position.

exercises.name: Pec Deck
exercises.description: Machine chest fly bringing the arms together in an arc, isolating the pectorals with constant cable-like tension throughout.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat
exercises.description: Barbell squat on a guided Smith machine providing a vertical bar path for safer high-volume quad and glute training.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.description: Angled sled machine squat emphasising the quadriceps through deep range of motion with reduced spinal compressive load.

exercises.name: Step Up
exercises.description: Unilateral step onto a box or bench under dumbbell load, building single-leg strength, hip stability, and quad development.

exercises.name: Nordic Curl
exercises.description: Eccentric hamstring movement anchoring the feet and lowering the torso toward the floor, building hamstring strength and preventing knee injuries.
